canonicalDefinition: To make something obscure, unclear, or unintelligible.
canonicalExamples: The contract was deliberately obfuscated.

canonicalDefinition: Done as a routine duty, without real interest or care.
canonicalExamples: He gave a perfunctory nod.

canonicalDefinition: Too great or extreme to be expressed in words.
canonicalExamples: The view inspired ineffable joy.

canonicalDefinition: To make a law or decision widely known.
canonicalExamples: New rules were promulgated in March.

canonicalDefinition: Stubbornly refusing to obey authority or accept guidance.
canonicalExamples: The committee dealt with a recalcitrant member.

canonicalDefinition: Apparently true, but not necessarily so.
canonicalExamples: He left ostensibly to find more coffee.

canonicalDefinition: A solution that is claimed to fix every problem.
canonicalExamples: Technology is not a panacea.

canonicalDefinition: Optimistic or confident, especially in a difficult situation.
canonicalExamples: She remained sanguine about the outcome.

canonicalDefinition: A change of circumstances or fortune, usually unwelcome.
canonicalExamples: The vicissitudes of life made her wiser.

canonicalDefinition: Extremely idealistic; unrealistic and impractical.
canonicalExamples: His quixotic plan never had a chance.

canonicalDefinition: Looking or sounding sad and dismal.
canonicalExamples: She told the story in a lugubrious tone.

canonicalDefinition: Excessively talkative, especially about trivial matters.
canonicalExamples: The garrulous host kept everyone listening.

canonicalDefinition: Things that exist or are remembered for only a short time.
canonicalExamples: The museum collects political ephemera.

canonicalDefinition: Having keen mental discernment; perceptive.
canonicalExamples: Her perspicacious remarks impressed the panel.

canonicalDefinition: Very generous or forgiving, especially toward a rival.
canonicalExamples: He was magnanimous in defeat.

canonicalDefinition: Noisy and difficult to control.
canonicalExamples: The obstreperous crowd refused to leave.

canonicalDefinition: Unable to be avoided or escaped.
canonicalExamples: The ineluctable conclusion is clear.

canonicalDefinition: Practicing severe self-discipline, avoiding indulgence.
canonicalExamples: He lived an ascetic life in the mountains.

canonicalDefinition: Deceitful and untrustworthy.
canonicalExamples: The perfidious advisor was finally exposed.

canonicalDefinition: A person who flatters powerful people for personal gain.
canonicalExamples: The king was surrounded by sycophants.

canonicalDefinition: To formally cancel a law or agreement.
canonicalExamples: The treaty was abrogated by the new government.

canonicalDefinition: Angry and bitter, especially in a dispute.
canonicalExamples: The divorce was acrimonious.

canonicalDefinition: Eager and prompt willingness to do something.
canonicalExamples: She accepted the offer with alacrity.

canonicalDefinition: Something that belongs to a different time period.
canonicalExamples: The smartphone in the medieval film is an anachronism.

canonicalDefinition: Deep dislike of someone or something.
canonicalExamples: She has an antipathy toward arrogant people.

canonicalDefinition: Self-confidence and composure under pressure.
canonicalExamples: She handled the question with aplomb.

canonicalDefinition: Of doubtful authenticity, although widely repeated.
canonicalExamples: The famous quote is apocryphal.

canonicalDefinition: Eager to fight or argue.
canonicalExamples: The senator gave a bellicose speech.

canonicalDefinition: Sounding important but with little real meaning.
canonicalExamples: His bombastic style annoyed the audience.

canonicalDefinition: A harsh mixture of sounds.
canonicalExamples: The street was a cacophony of horns.

canonicalDefinition: Young and inexperienced.
canonicalExamples: He was a callow youth at the time.

canonicalDefinition: Use of trickery to achieve a purpose.
canonicalExamples: The lawyer was accused of chicanery.

canonicalDefinition: Rude and unfriendly.
canonicalExamples: It would be churlish to refuse.

canonicalDefinition: A political leader who appeals to emotions and prejudice.
canonicalExamples: The crowd cheered the demagogue.

canonicalDefinition: To criticize unfairly; to damage someone's reputation.
canonicalExamples: She refused to denigrate her opponents.

canonicalDefinition: Lacking purpose or enthusiasm; jumping from one thing to another.
canonicalExamples: He gave a desultory effort.

canonicalDefinition: Slow to act, intended to cause delay.
canonicalExamples: His dilatory tactics frustrated the judge.

canonicalDefinition: Cheerful and full of energy.
canonicalExamples: She was ebullient after the win.

canonicalDefinition: Outstandingly bad; shocking.
canonicalExamples: An egregious error in the report.

canonicalDefinition: A perfect example of something.
canonicalExamples: She is the epitome of elegance.

canonicalDefinition: Soon passing out of sight or memory.
canonicalExamples: The evanescent glow of the sunset.

canonicalDefinition: Very attentive to detail and hard to please.
canonicalExamples: He is fastidious about his appearance.

canonicalDefinition: To deny or contradict.
canonicalExamples: No one can gainsay her results.

canonicalDefinition: Excessive pride or self-confidence.
canonicalExamples: Their hubris led to their downfall.

canonicalDefinition: Having little or no money.
canonicalExamples: The impecunious artist lived simply.

canonicalDefinition: Tireless; never giving up.
canonicalExamples: She is an indefatigable campaigner.

canonicalDefinition: Slow and relaxed; lacking energy.
canonicalExamples: She moved with a languid grace.

canonicalDefinition: Pleasingly smooth and musical to hear.
canonicalExamples: He has a mellifluous voice.

canonicalDefinition: Trying too hard to please by being overly obedient.
canonicalExamples: The waiter was obsequious.

canonicalDefinition: Transparently clear in meaning or appearance.
canonicalExamples: A pellucid explanation.
